sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् । तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जिस समय सत्त्वगुण बढ़ा हो, उस समय यदि देहधारी मनुष्य मर जाता है, तो वह,उत्तमवेत्ताओंके निर्मल लोकोंमें जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: When sattva is predominant at death, the embodied being reaches the pure worlds of the wise.
essence: A clear state carries consciousness toward a clearer destination.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: If a person dies while sattva is strongest, that being reaches pure realms associated with the wise.
simpleInsight: Your dominant state matters most at the end; it shapes where consciousness moves next.
detailedMeaning: Krishna continues explaining the three gunas to Arjuna on the battlefield. After naming the signs of rajas and tamas, he shows the quieter power of sattva: if death comes while clarity is strongest, the being moves toward pure worlds, not confusion.
modernRelevance: You are exhausted after a long day, and your mind is either clear or clouded when the hardest conversation arrives. The state you live in most deeply leaves the strongest imprint.
translationLiteral: यदा (when) / सत्त्वे (in sattva) / प्रवृद्धे (having become strong) / तु (indeed) / प्रलयम् (dissolution; death) / याति (goes) / देहभृत् (the embodied one) / तदा (then) / उत्तमविदाम् (of the knowers of the highest) / लोकान् (worlds) / अमलान् (pure, stainless) / प्रतिपद्यते (attains)
insight: It shifts attention from isolated actions to the quality of mind that dominates your life.
